title: Christine Ing
content:
People.ContactEmail christineing@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
- University of Toronto
Christine syncs technology with the law, combining analytical reasoning with innovative solutions to resolve clients’ most complex tech problems.
Christine is an equity partner and leader of the firm’s National Technology Law Group and co-leader of the FinTech Group. She is a trusted, strategic advisor to some of the most well-respected companies in Canada. She is known for her ability to guide complex, high-stakes technology and data focused transactions with precision and insight.  She is well-known in the market for her ability to handle complex, innovative deals and deliver practical solutions that balance risk and innovation.
Christine’s is an expert in the areas of information technology, data security and intellectual property.   For over twenty five years, she has helped clients in connection with complex corporate and commercial transactions, including outsourcing, cloud computing, SaaS and other strategic procurement arrangements, software development and systems implementation, licensing, data sharing and joint ventures. She has particular expertise in the areas of information security and third party and fourth party vendor risk management.
Christine has assisted clients across a broad spectrum of industry sectors, with a focus on financial services and other regulated industries. She has broad experience in the payments space, having handled a variety of matters relating to credit and debit cards, loyalty programs, BNPL and global transaction banking. She is increasingly being engaged on strategic deals centred on agentic AI, passkeys and identity.
Christine is well-recognized in numerous industry publications in multiple fields, including information technology, data protection and privacy, financial services regulatory and patent law.  Her rankings include Chambers Global and Chambers Canada (Technology Band 1) , The Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory (Leading Lawyer: Computer & IT Law; Data Protection & Privacy; Technology Transactions); Lexpert’s Leading 500 Cross-border Lawyers (Leading Lawyer: Computer & IT Law); Best Lawyers in Canada (Information Technology; Intellectual Property; Privacy and Data Security; Technology Law; Lawyer of the Year (Technology Law) and Lawyer of the Year (Information Technology Law)); The Legal 500 Canada (Hall of Fame: Technology); IFLR1000 Guide to the World’s Leading Financial Law Firms (Highly Regarded: Financial Services Regulatory); and Intellectual Asset Management (IAM) Patent 1000 (Leading Lawyer: Patent Transactions) .
- Structuring and negotiating two-way data sharing agreements between collaboration partners in relation to data of mutual customers
- Advising financial institutions in relation to procuring AI - powered SaaS solutions from foreign service providers and managing and mitigating the associated information security and privacy risks
- Counselling a number of financial institutions in relation to payments modernization in Canada
- Assisting banks in negotiating credit and debit card manufacturing and supply arrangements
- Assisting a multi-national bank in connection with its core commercial arrangements with its payment network providers
- Advising a number of financial institutions in relation to the administration of financial benchmarks in Canada
- Advising a life insurance provider in relation to multiple vendor agreements central to the modernization of its online application and adjudication processes
- Advising an auto insurance provider in relation to the launch of a mobile telematics app
- Assisting a U.K.-based mobile app provider in the healthcare space with its expansion into Canada and the negotiation of a long-term, exclusive reseller and collaboration agreement in Canada
- Advising a foreign provider of healthcare solutions in relation to the divestiture of its Canadian business and the structuring and negotiation of a long-term, exclusive reseller agreement in Canada
- Acting for a leading Canadian pharmaceutical company on the structuring and negotiation of a long-term collaboration agreement for the development and commercialization of a drug for the treatment of certain neurological conditions, including the securing of key patent and technology licences from a large US-based biotech company
- Advising a U.S.-based outsourcing service provider in relation to a multi-year, high value outsourcing agreement with provincial health authorities
Christine holds a Bachelor of Science in Engineering from the University of Toronto, 1990, and obtained her LLB from Osgoode Hall Law School, 1993. She was called to the Ontario bar in 1995.
- Guest speaker, “AI for General Counsels and Legal Departments”, General Counsel of Publicly-Listed, Family-Controlled Canadian Companies, June 25, 2024
- Guest speaker, “Annual Update on Contract Drafting” Program, Ontario Bar Association, March 19, 2024
- Guest speaker, "Forum on Data Governance & Ethics", Cambridge Forums, November 28, 2023
- Guest speaker, "Outsourcing 2023: Fundamentals, Key Issues, and Best Practices" Program, Practising Law Institute, October 26, 2023
- Guest speaker, "Accelerate AI" Program - "Accelerate the AI Contracting Process: Tips from the Trenches", Vector Institute, July 6, 2021
- Guest speaker, "Board Oversight of Artificial Intelligence" Course, Institute of Corporate Directors, May 11, 2021
